AI Generated ImageOne of the most common interview questions for Angular developers is:
“Should I use Default or OnPush change detection?”
Many developers answer this by saying “Use OnPush because it’s faster.” While that’s partly true, it’s an oversimplification. The right choice depends on your application’s architecture, data flow, and how state is managed.
In this article, we’ll explore:

• What Angular Change Detection is

• Default vs OnPush Change Detection

• A real-world issue caused by using Default incorrectly

• A real-world issue caused by using OnPush incorrectly

• Best practices

• How Angular 22 approaches change detection
What is Change Detection?
Angular keeps the UI synchronized with your application’s data through a mechanism called Change Detection.
Whenever application state changes, Angular checks whether the template needs to be updated.
For example:
@Component({
selector: 'app-user'
})
export class UserComponent {
user = {
name: 'Mathew'
};updateName() {…
